---
title: "Pipelines"
description: "End-to-end classification and clustering orchestration"
---

## Overview

MLCore provides two pipeline entry points that compose all lower-level components into single-call workflows. Both pipelines follow the same pattern: configure â†’ run â†’ check result.

## Classification Pipeline

`ClassificationPipeline` (`pipelines/ClassificationPipeline.hpp`) orchestrates supervised classification end-to-end.

### Pipeline Stages

For frame-based models (Random Forest, Naive Bayes, Logistic Regression):

```
ValidatingFeatures â†’ ConvertingFeatures â†’ AssemblingLabels â†’
BalancingClasses â†’ Training â†’ Predicting â†’ ComputingMetrics â†’ WritingOutput â†’ Complete
```

For sequence models (HMM):

```
ValidatingFeatures â†’ ConvertingFeatures â†’ AssemblingLabels â†’
BalancingClasses â†’ SegmentingSequences â†’ Training â†’ Predicting â†’
ComputingMetrics â†’ WritingOutput â†’ Complete
```

The `SegmentingSequences` stage uses `SequenceAssembler::segment()` to split the feature matrix and labels into contiguous temporal segments (based on gaps in `TimeFrameIndex`). Training then routes through `trainSequences()` and prediction through `predictSequences()`, preserving temporal structure for models like HMMs.

### Configuration

```cpp
ClassificationPipelineConfig config;

// Model
config.model_name = "Random Forest";
config.model_params = &rf_params;  // or nullptr for defaults

// Features
config.feature_tensor_key = "ml_features";

// Labels (variant â€” pick one mode)
config.label_config = LabelFromIntervals{"Contact", "No Contact"};
config.label_interval_key = "contact_intervals";

// Conversion
config.conversion_config.zscore_normalize = true;

// Balancing (optional)
config.balance_classes = true;
config.balancing_config.strategy = BalancingStrategy::Subsample;

// Prediction region
config.prediction_region.predict_all_rows = true;
// Optional: filter output to a specific interval (runs Viterbi on full span
// for temporal context, writes only frames within the interval)
// config.prediction_region.prediction_interval_key = "target_region";
// config.prediction_region.constrained_decoding = true; // HMM warm-start

// Output
config.output_config.output_prefix = "Predicted:";
config.time_key_str = "camera";
```

### Entry Point

```cpp
auto result = runClassificationPipeline(dm, registry, config, progress_callback);

if (result.success) {
    // result.training_observations, result.num_classes
    // result.binary_train_metrics or result.multi_class_train_metrics
    // result.writer_result contains created DataManager keys
    // result.trained_model can be reused or saved
}
```

### Result Fields

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `success` | Whether the pipeline completed |
| `error_message` | Human-readable error (if failed) |
| `failed_stage` | Which stage failed |
| `training_observations` | Count after NaN dropping and balancing |
| `num_features` / `num_classes` | Training dimensions |
| `class_names` | From label assembly |
| `rows_dropped_nan` | NaN/Inf rows removed |
| `was_balanced` | Whether balancing was applied |
| `binary_train_metrics` | Accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, F1, confusion matrix |
| `multi_class_train_metrics` | Per-class precision/recall/F1, overall accuracy |
| `prediction_observations` | Count of predicted frames |
| `writer_result` | Created DataManager keys, group IDs |
| `trained_model` | Ownership transferred â€” reusable or saveable |

### Progress Callback

```cpp
auto progress = [](ClassificationStage stage, std::string const & msg) {
    spdlog::info("Pipeline: {} â€” {}", toString(stage), msg);
};
```

## Clustering Pipeline

`ClusteringPipeline` (`pipelines/ClusteringPipeline.hpp`) orchestrates unsupervised clustering end-to-end.

### Pipeline Stages

```
ValidatingFeatures â†’ ConvertingFeatures â†’ Fitting â†’
AssigningClusters â†’ WritingOutput â†’ Complete
```

### Configuration

```cpp
ClusteringPipelineConfig config;

// Model
config.model_name = "K-Means";
config.model_params = &kmeans_params;

// Features
config.feature_tensor_key = "pca_features";

// Conversion
config.conversion_config.zscore_normalize = true;

// Assignment region
config.assignment_region.assign_all_rows = true;

// Output
config.output_config.output_prefix = "Cluster:";
config.output_config.write_to_putative_groups = true;
config.output_config.write_intervals = true;
config.output_config.write_probabilities = true;  // GMM only
config.time_key_str = "camera";
```

### Entry Point

```cpp
auto result = runClusteringPipeline(dm, registry, config, progress_callback);

if (result.success) {
    // result.num_clusters, result.cluster_sizes, result.cluster_names
    // result.interval_keys â€” created DigitalIntervalSeries keys
    // result.probability_keys â€” created AnalogTimeSeries keys (GMM)
    // result.putative_group_ids â€” created entity group IDs
    // result.fitted_model â€” reusable
}
```

### Result Fields

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `success` | Whether the pipeline completed |
| `error_message` | Human-readable error (if failed) |
| `fitting_observations` | Count after NaN dropping |
| `num_features` / `num_clusters` | Fitting dimensions |
| `noise_points` | DBSCAN noise count (0 for others) |
| `cluster_sizes` | Per-cluster observation counts |
| `cluster_names` | `"{prefix}{index}"` format |
| `interval_keys` | Created `DigitalIntervalSeries` DataManager keys |
| `probability_keys` | Created `AnalogTimeSeries` keys (GMM soft assignments) |
| `putative_group_ids` | Created entity group IDs |
| `fitted_model` | Ownership transferred â€” reusable |

## Error Handling

Both pipelines use early-return on failure. The `failed_stage` field indicates where the problem occurred. Common failure modes:

- **ValidatingFeatures**: Feature tensor not found, not 2D, or empty
- **ConvertingFeatures**: All rows dropped due to NaN/Inf
- **AssemblingLabels**: No labels found, incompatible row types
- **SegmentingSequences**: No contiguous sequences meeting minimum length
- **Training/Fitting**: Model failed internally (rare with valid data)
- **WritingOutput**: DataManager write failure

## Bounding Span (Prediction Region Filtering)

`BoundingSpan.hpp` (`pipelines/BoundingSpan.hpp`) provides utilities for computing and applying temporal bounding spans when prediction is restricted to a specific interval.

### Motivation

When a user selects a prediction interval (e.g., "predict only frames 500â€“800"), sequence models like HMMs still need temporal context beyond those frames to avoid cold-start inaccuracy at boundaries. The bounding span covers the union of all training label intervals and prediction intervals, so the model sees the full relevant temporal range. Output is later filtered to only the requested prediction frames.

### API

| Function | Description |
|---|---|
| `computeIntervalBounds(series)` | Returns `[min(start), max(end)]` of all intervals in a `DigitalIntervalSeries` |
| `computeRowTimeBounds(times)` | Returns `[min, max]` of a `vector<TimeFrameIndex>` |
| `mergeSpans(a, b)` | Returns the union span covering both inputs |
| `filterRowsToSpan(features, times, span)` | Filters feature matrix columns and time vector to only those within the span |

### Pipeline Integration

In `ClassificationPipeline`, when `prediction_interval_key` is non-empty:

**Stage 6 (Predict) â€” Bounding span:**

1. Load the prediction interval series from DataManager
2. Compute its bounds; optionally merge with training label interval bounds
3. Filter `predict_features` and `predict_row_times` to the merged bounding span
4. Prediction proceeds on this filtered (but temporally complete) range

**Stage 7 (Metrics)** runs on the full bounding-span predictions (unfiltered), giving training-set accuracy.

**Stage 8 (Output) â€” Output filtering:**

1. `filterPredictionsToIntervals()` filters predictions, probabilities, and times to only frames within the prediction intervals
2. The filtered output is passed to `PredictionWriter`, so DataManager only receives results for the user-requested region

This two-step design ensures sequence models get full temporal context during prediction, while output is limited to the frames the user actually requested.

| Function | Description |
|---|---|
| `filterPredictionsToIntervals(preds, probs, times, intervals)` | Keeps only frames that fall within any interval in the series |
